topic: Tidal O1 period — verification of c4 patches (4 patches landed)
our_argument_summary: WIN-050 (Tidal O1 period). Verdict: Self-Contradicted. We argue the dome adopts the textbook 25.82 h O1 period without derivation; the dome model claims O1 as a confirmed prediction but its own geometry (moon at 2,534 km above a 20,015 km disc) cannot produce the smooth, latitude-dependent diurnal modulation O1 requires — the close-moon geometry produces a sharp tidal pulse beneath the sub-lunar track, dropping to ~9% of peak at 5,000 km offset and ~1.48% at the dome equator (10,000 km from sub-lunar point) under a Newtonian 1/r³ falloff baseline. The new c4 burden-shifting sentence pre-empts the obvious aetheric-falloff dodge. Dome has no monitor.py entry for O1; the value is post-hoc adoption of standard tidal physics; the verdict also fails our trivial-transcription test (Part 3.1b).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No O1-specific code path. monitor.py contains no domain for O1 (verified via grep — zero hits for O1, tidal, tide, or 25.82). The dashboard tidal entries cover only M2 / K1 / S2 (raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 212, 216, 240). O1 appears in WIN list only.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — the WIN has no automation at all; the period is stated as matching observation, not computed.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: N/A — no monitor entry, no comparison logic.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The detail_verdict_text correctly states 'no monitor.py entry exists for this tidal constituent at all' — confirmed via grep. The trivial-transcription test framing (Part 3.1b) is correctly applied since no timestamp document exists either.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: raw-text/08-live-power.txt lists tidal domains M2 (line 212), K1 (216), S2 (240) but not O1. monitor.py has zero hits for O1, 25.82, tidal, or tide. No automation, no comparison.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: O1 is a textbook constituent of the Doodson (1921) harmonic tidal expansion. The dome adopts the 25.82 h period verbatim and offers no alternative numerical prediction or independent derivation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Doodson 1921 catalogued O1 (25.819 h) over a century before the dome model existed. The V51.0 WIN is the published value re-stated; no derivation, timestamp, or pre-data prediction document exists.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No raw-text or dome-site derivation connects O1 to dome parameters (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, moon_altitude=2,534 km). Period is stated, not computed from disc geometry.

patch_verification.summary: All 4 c4 patches confirmed landed in detail_evidence. None reverted. No regressions detected.

patches.verbatim_text_present: the burden is on the dome to specify a propagation law that produces O1's observed latitude-dependent amplitude without this falloff
patches.notes: Sentence sits between the 1.48% figure and the declination paragraph — exactly the recommended position.

patches.notes: Adopted the recommended hedged form in detail_evidence — but see new hole NH-1 below: TLDR_evidence was NOT updated to match and still says 389 unhedged.

patches.description: Opening sentence tightened to specify mechanism
patches.verbatim_text_present: O1 (25.82 hours) is the principal lunar diurnal constituent — it arises from the moon's declination cycle interacting with Earth's rotation, not merely from the lunar orbital period

holes_found.description: TLDR/detail Doodson-count inconsistency introduced by partial patch. The c4 patch hedged the Doodson count from '389' to '~390 (62 in practice)' in detail_evidence, but tldr_evidence still asserts the unhedged form: 'the same Doodson expansion that catalogs 389 tidal frequencies'. A reader who only reads the TLDR sees a confidently-wrong specific number; a reader who expands sees the hedge. A dome defender who notices the gap can argue 'your own review can't agree with itself on a basic citation'. The gap is small (389 vs ~390) but it carries forward the original c4/c3/c2 concern.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch tldr_evidence: replace 'the same Doodson expansion that catalogs 389 tidal frequencies' with 'the same Doodson expansion that catalogs hundreds of tidal frequencies' (or '~390'). Either restating the hedge or removing the specific number cleans up the inconsistency.

holes_found.description: Section 2.2 cross-link is anchored imprecisely. detail_evidence ends with a link whose href is '#part2' and onclick switches to the selftest tab. The anchor '#part2' jumps to the top of the Self-Tests tab, but the actual subsection 2.2 (Tidal Pattern: One Spike vs. Two Bulges) has its own anchor '#2-2-tidal-pattern-one-spike-vs-two-bulges' (verified in data/sections.json:part2.html). The link works (correct tab), but lands the reader above the relevant subsection rather than at it. Minor UX, not a fact problem.
holes_found.recommendation: Change the href from '#part2' to '#2-2-tidal-pattern-one-spike-vs-two-bulges' while keeping the existing onclick=showTab('selftest');return false. Single-token edit, cannot regress anything.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model is correct that O1 is a real tidal constituent with period 25.82 h, and that this number can be produced by any model where the moon orbits the Earth at the right cadence — period alone is geometry-agnostic in the way the dome implies (data/wins.json:050.detail_evidence opening sentence after c4 patch). The c4 opening-sentence patch tightened our framing precisely because the dome's point about period-as-confirmation is not entirely empty: a moon at any altitude that completes the right declination cycle will produce something at this frequency.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Period-matching is necessary but nowhere near sufficient. O1 is identified observationally not by its period alone but by its smooth, latitude-dependent amplitude pattern — a signature that requires a distant moon (d/R ≫ 1) so the tidal force is approximately uniform across the Earth's surface (data/wins.json:050.detail_evidence). The dome's d/R ratio (moon_altitude=2,534 km / disc_radius=20,015 km ≈ 0.13, per CLAUDE.md core dome parameters) is the inverse regime: tidal force is sharply peaked beneath the sub-lunar track, dropping to ~1.48% of peak at the dome equator (computed in detail_evidence). Reproducing the period without reproducing the amplitude pattern is exactly the kind of trivial-transcription win our Part 3.1b test is designed to catch (data/sections.json:part3 anchor #posthoc-standard, verified to exist this run).

deeper_analysis:
Verification pass on the four c4 patches. All landed cleanly in detail_evidence with no regressions, no verdict drift, and the burden-shifting sentence sits in the right structural position to neutralize the strongest dome-defender comeback ('but Newtonian 1/r³ doesn't apply to aetheric coupling'). The two new minor holes are both consequences of patches landing in detail_evidence but not propagating to TLDR_evidence (Doodson count) and not refining the cross-link anchor — both common patterns when a moderate-severity edit pulls focus from polish-level cleanup. Neither would be visible to the c4 reviewer because they didn't exist until the patch landed. The Section 2.2 anchor issue may exist on other WINs that cross-link into Part 2 — worth a holistic spot-check sometime, but not blocking this verification.

Advocate-mode (other side): a dome defender's strongest comeback to this WIN is now neutered by the burden-shift sentence. The remaining defender move is 'period-matching is geometry-agnostic so don't blame us for a textbook value' — which is technically true and which our updated opening sentence concedes; the rebuttal (period without amplitude pattern is trivial transcription) holds firm. The 1.48% number itself is computed under one specific assumption (sub-lunar point at disc center, dome equator at r=10,000 km); a defender could quibble that the moon's circuit traces a small annulus rather than parking over the center, so 'the equator' point sometimes sees a closer moon. This is true but doesn't help the defender — under any sub-lunar location, the d/R ratio remains ~0.13 and the falloff is still steep. The 1.48% is illustrative of the regime, not a precise pointwise prediction; the prose presents it as illustrative, not as a falsification target, so this is fine as written.

citations_verified: Doodson 1921 DOI 10.1098/rspa.1921.0088 (in-prose link, Proc. Roy. Soc. A — standard tidal-analysis source)
moon_altitude=2,534 km (matches CLAUDE.md core dome parameters)
disc_radius=20,015 km (matches CLAUDE.md core dome parameters)
Section 3.1b anchor #posthoc-standard exists (data/sections.json:part3.html, grepped this run)
9% at 5,000 km and 1.48% at equator arithmetic: under d=sqrt(r^2+2534^2), tidal force ∝ 1/d^3 — (2534/5605)^3 = 0.0925, (2534/10318)^3 = 0.0148, both check

summary_for_decider: All 4 c4 patches landed cleanly. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) holds; confidence 0.9. Two new minor holes both downstream of the patch landings: (1) tldr_evidence Doodson count not updated to match hedged detail ('389' vs '~390'); (2) 'Section 2.2' cross-link anchored to '#part2' instead of '#2-2-tidal-pattern-one-spike-vs-two-bulges'. Both are single-token edits and can be self-applied. No verdict change. No carry-over from prior cycles. Batched (position 1 of 2) — see WIN-053.c10.json for batch position 2.
